teachingPoint: Platform generates flows for master tenant already; admin of master tenant = super admin. A new identity constant introduces a parallel identity space with no additional semantic distinction. Reuse existing MASTER scope.
negativeExample: New PLATFORM_AGENT_ID constant separate from MASTER_TENANT_ID
discriminatingConstraint: Use existing MASTER_TENANT_ID; do not introduce parallel identity space
keywords: master tenant agent identity platform scope

teachingPoint: pendingActions is the tenant's existing mental model for 'things waiting for my approval'. Agent proposes a new action TYPE, not a new protocol.
discriminatingConstraint: Reuse existing approval protocol; introduce only a new action type
keywords: approval pending actions confirmation rollback tenant

teachingPoint: Agent-private: PRIVATE+MASTER. Agent-for-tenant: PRIVATE+targetTenant. Platform template: GLOBAL+MASTER. Three cases, two scopes, one identity discriminator — sufficient.
negativeExample: Add knowledgeScope: 'PLATFORM' between PRIVATE and GLOBAL
discriminatingConstraint: Use existing two-scope model; no new scope types
keywords: scope private global platform tenant isolation knowledge

teachingPoint: DRAFT is the designed state for unapproved work. Skipping DRAFT = skipping the tenant's consent window. promoteDraft() is the gating mechanism.
negativeExample: Direct write to tenant's PUBLISHED flow under the agent's admin identity
discriminatingConstraint: Mutations to tenant PUBLISHED flows go through DRAFT; promoteDraft is the consent gate
keywords: draft propose edit promote consent tenant fork

teachingPoint: Platform authority is platform-scoped. Requiring tenant consent for platform-level content collapses the distinction between tenant and platform.
positiveExample: APPLY_GLOBAL with no tenant approval; MASTER IS the platform
negativeExample: Tenant approval for platform-wide template changes
discriminatingConstraint: Platform template mutations do not require tenant consent
keywords: apply global platform template master authority consent

teachingPoint: AF-9 is tenant-scoped quality; super judge is platform-scoped quality. Different concerns, different context scopes. Replacing AF-9 loses the tenant-scoped signal.
discriminatingConstraint: Super judge augments; does not replace AF-9
keywords: super judge af-9 arbiter placement post convergence platform

teachingPoint: A judge with zero evidence is a random number generator. Hallucinated verdicts DEGRADE the training pipeline, not improve it. Zero-cost DEFER is the correctness path, not an optimization.
positiveExample: DEFER_TO_AF9 instantly with zero LLM call (CF-840)
negativeExample: Always invoke LLM; low-evidence verdicts still useful as signal
discriminatingConstraint: platformPatternsMatched === 0 → zero LLM; quality requires evidence
keywords: zero cost defer super judge evidence llm hallucination

teachingPoint: MACHINE rule: no hardcoded model names. Operator authority extends to model choice. FREEDOM/MACHINE distinction means operator chooses the highest-tier model they are willing to pay for.
discriminatingConstraint: Model selection via FREEDOM config; never hardcoded
keywords: freedom config model super judge operator hardcoded

teachingPoint: Agent-pure solutions belong to the platform (no tenant data existed to consent to). Tenant-derived solutions may encode tenant-specific logic requiring consent. Two paths match two different provenances.
positiveExample: Path A (agent-pure, immediate) + Path B (tenant-derived, consent-gated)
negativeExample: Single-path model — all contributions go through consent gate
discriminatingConstraint: Contribution path selection based on solution provenance
keywords: contribution path provenance consent tenant derived agent pure

teachingPoint: If no tenant data was used to produce the solution, there is no tenant data to consent to. MASTER IS the platform; agent-pure outputs ARE platform content.
positiveExample: DD-323: logic travels, data does not. Path A writes directly without consent
negativeExample: Require consent for all contributions regardless of provenance
discriminatingConstraint: DD-323 logic-only principle — provenance determines consent requirement
keywords: dd-323 logic data tenant provenance agent pure contribute

teachingPoint: Privacy invariants are strongest when enforced within a single service. Cross-service privacy contracts create ambiguity about which side of the boundary is responsible. CF-841 is a LOCAL invariant — enforce it where the invariant lives.
positiveExample: PatternSanitizer as internal method of T655 PatternContributor
discriminatingConstraint: Privacy-critical logic internal to the service that owns the invariant
keywords: sanitizer privacy boundary internal method service cf-841

teachingPoint: Agent has broader evidence (platform patterns + cross-tenant context); its overrides represent more meaningful disagreements. Treat agent-sourced DPO with higher quality weighting in M3 fine-tuning.
positiveExample: Cross-tenant context makes chosen/rejected more discriminating
negativeExample: Treat agent and tenant DPO triples identically
discriminatingConstraint: DPO quality weighted by evidence breadth (cross-tenant context = higher weight)
keywords: dpo agent quality training m3 evidence discriminating weight

teachingPoint: Audit-before-switch is a tenant-isolation invariant: the audit record must outlive the scope it describes. Audits written inside a switched scope are invisible to the platform admin who authorized the switch.
ironRules: xiigen-agent-actions audit record written BEFORE cls.run (CF-839)
cls.run + cls.set pattern; manual restoration forbidden
outer MASTER scope restored by try/finally inside ClsService
codeSnippet: await this.db.storeDocument('xiigen-agent-actions', audit, id); return this.cls.run(async () => { this.cls.set(TENANT_CONTEXT_KEY, targetCtx); return await inner(); });
keywords: cls audit scope switch tenant isolation master

teachingPoint: A judge with no evidence emits zero verdicts. Zero-LLM fast-fail is not an optimization — it is the correctness path. Hallucinated verdicts degrade the training pipeline downstream.
ironRules: No LLM call on zero-evidence path; cost=0
DEFER_TO_AF9 path does NOT write a DPO triple
keywords: zero cost llm defer evidence judge arbiter gate

teachingPoint: Privacy-critical logic internal to the service that owns the invariant creates a local guarantee. Cross-service privacy contracts introduce ambiguity about which side is responsible for the invariant.
ironRules: PatternSanitizer is an internal method of T655 — not a separate service
CF-841: sanitizer failure → abort + status=SANITIZATION_FAILED + no retry
Pre-sanitization fields preserved in audit record (xiigen-agent-contributions)
codeSnippet: const { sanitized, stripped } = this.sanitize(solution); if (!sanitized) { await this.auditFailure(sessionId, solution); return DataProcessResult.failure('SANITIZATION_FAILED'); }
keywords: sanitizer privacy internal method boundary invariant local
